title: OpenAM Session Service RPC GetSessionCount exposes arbitrary users' sessions (Improper Authorization)
description: OpenAM's stateful Session Service RPC handler (SessionRequestHandler.processMethod in openam-core) implements the GetSessionCount request method by reading an attacker-supplied universal identifier from req.getUUID() and calling sessionQueryManager.getAllSessionsByUUID(uuid), returning the full map of active sessions (including session identifiers/credentials) for that UUID via res.setSessionsForGivenUUID(sessions). The only access check performed before this method runs (verifyValidRequest) merely verifies the requester's own session is not a restricted token; it never checks that the requester owns the target UUID or holds administrative privilege. As a result, any low-privileged authenticated user who can reach the session endpoint and who knows another user's identity identifier can retrieve that user's active session credentials, enabling session hijacking and, if a privileged account is targeted, privilege escalation (CWE-285 / CWE-200).
